title: Selliguea albopes
taxonomy: Selliguea albopes belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as polypody ferns. This plant is a type of epiphytic fern, meaning it grows on other plants or objects without deriving nutrients from them. Selliguea albopes is a species within the genus Selliguea, which contains a number of fern species found in tropical regions. The plant's classification can also be broken down further into the class Polypodiopsida and the division Pteridophyta. Selliguea albopes is native to tropical regions and is often found in shaded, humid environments.

intro: Selliguea albopunctata, commonly known as the white-spotted polypody, is a species of fern native to tropical Asia. The health benefits of Selliguea albopunctata include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
